assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and can receive email when the selected route requires it.
No platform account, tenant, credentials, or product resources exist yet.
Developer has access to an API key or can create one

developer_goal: Send an email programmatically using the SendGrid API.

first_success.outcome: Developer sends a valid email message through the SendGrid API and receives a successful response.
first_success.observable_signal: HTTP 202 Accepted response status code from the API indicating the email was accepted for delivery.

route_selection.rule: Reconstruct complete official candidate routes, prefer no payment/admin/approval when possible, then minimize required actions; leave consequential ties unresolved.

candidates.name: SendGrid UI
candidates.reason: The UI is for manual email campaign management and not the primary developer onboarding surface for API integration.

candidates.name: SendGrid SDKs
candidates.reason: SDKs are wrappers around the Web API; the API itself is the fundamental integration surface.

required_path.action: Sign up for a SendGrid account.

required_fields.label: User registration information.
required_fields.notes: Mapped from machine-drafted primary_path; field inventory not independently audited.

required_path.action: Enable Two-factor authentication (2FA) on the account.

required_fields.label: Phone number or Authy app setup.
required_fields.notes: Mapped from machine-drafted primary_path; field inventory not independently audited.

required_path.action: Create an API key with Mail Send > Full Access permissions.

required_fields.label: API key name and permissions.
required_fields.notes: Mapped from machine-drafted primary_path; field inventory not independently audited.

required_path.action: Complete Domain Authentication to verify sender identity.

required_fields.label: Domain DNS configuration.
required_fields.notes: Mapped from machine-drafted primary_path; field inventory not independently audited.

required_path.action: Set environment variable SENDGRID_API_KEY with the API key value.

required_fields.label: API key string.
required_fields.notes: Mapped from machine-drafted primary_path; field inventory not independently audited.

required_path.action: Write code to send an email using the SendGrid Mail Send API or SDK.

required_fields.label: Email message parameters and API key.
required_fields.notes: Mapped from machine-drafted primary_path; field inventory not independently audited.

required_path.action: Run the code or curl command to send the email.

required_fields.label: Compiled or interpreted code or curl command.
required_fields.notes: Mapped from machine-drafted primary_path; field inventory not independently audited.

required_path.observable_result: Email received in recipient inbox or HTTP 202 response.

prerequisites.description: Create a SendGrid account to access the API and dashboard.
Enable Two-factor authentication (2FA) on the SendGrid account.
Create and store a SendGrid API Key with Mail Send > Full Access permissions.
Complete Domain Authentication to verify sender identity for production use.
Install required programming language SDK or have tools to make HTTP requests (e.g., curl).

external_gates.description: Must create a SendGrid account to access API and dashboard.
Two-factor authentication (2FA) is required to secure the account.
API key with Mail Send permissions must be created and stored securely.
Domain Authentication is required to verify sender identity for production use.
Developer must have programming environment and optionally install SendGrid SDK.

platform_outcomes.description: Successfully send the first email using the SendGrid Mail Send API.

sources.title: Getting started with the SendGrid API
evidence_supported: Getting started with the SendGrid API

uncertainties.question: What is the typical or average time to first success (sending first email) as claimed by SendGrid?
uncertainties.impact: Without a vendor-claimed or documented time estimate, it is unclear how quickly a new developer can expect to send their first email.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: The official documentation does not provide explicit timing or duration estimates for onboarding or first email send.

uncertainties.question: Has the machine-drafted Twilio SendGrid route been independently shortest-path audited?
uncertainties.impact: Action counts and peer comparison must stay withheld until a human audit passes.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Complete a shortest-path audit against current official docs and mark verified only when evidence supports it.
